response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack web developer (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) but lacks the core requirements for this embedded networking security role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel modification experience, no embedded firmware or LAN-switch background, and no exposure to the listed security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) or L2/L3 networking protocols. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments (2014-2015) is far too dated and limited to substitute for the required strong C/UNIX systems expertise.
strengths: 15+ years of software development experience well exceeding the 3-year minimum
Some historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on a developer-tools product
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing advocacy
concerns: No indication of Japanese language proficiency, which is a native-level hard requirement
No embedded firmware, LAN-switch, or network security domain experience (MACsec, 802.1X, kernel modification)
Primary stack is web/cloud (TypeScript, Node, MongoDB) — misaligned with C-based embedded systems work

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking / switch security background
Insufficient recent C/UNIX and Linux kernel experience
